_meta.purpose: Atlanta-area events and patterns Bolo Boys plans around. Two categories: competing_events (drain audience away — porchfests, festivals, conventions) and boost_windows (holidays, three-day weekends, and patterns that increase demand). Used by the Pipeline status review to flag scene-level conflicts and by the Propose-dates workflow to identify good candidate dates for venue negotiations.

audience_overlap_levels.high: Major draw that significantly pulls audience away from competing shows in the same scene/neighborhood radius.
audience_overlap_levels.medium: Notable draw; competes for some audience but not catastrophic.
audience_overlap_levels.low: Niche or geographically distant; minimal overlap with Bolo Boys audiences.

boost_levels.high: Strong demand window — people are out, looking for entertainment, three-day weekends, marquee holiday.
boost_levels.medium: Soft demand bump — single holiday day, themed-show opportunity.

competing_events.name: Summer Shade Festival
competing_events.pattern: Late-August Sat–Sun (24th annual is 2026-08-22/23 per Eventeny + Adventures in Atlanta — historical pattern was 'last weekend of August' but this year it's the prior weekend)
competing_events.notes: Major draw for Grant Park / Beltline / Cabbagetown audiences. GPC's flagship music festival. 2026 dates confirmed 2026-05-21 via web research: Saturday 2026-08-22 (10 AM) through Sunday 2026-08-23 (6 PM) at Grant Park. BOOKED 2026-06-27: Bolo Boys play the gazebo stage Sat 8/22, 12:15–1:00 PM (via Don) — this is now a Bolo Boys show (see events.json summer-shade-festival-2026-08-22), not just a competing event. The Bolo Boys 2026-08-29 Side Saddle show is the weekend AFTER Summer Shade — NOT a conflict.

competing_events.name: Virginia Highland Porchfest
competing_events.notes: Huge intown draw — ticketed starting 2026. Pulls heavily from Atlanta intown audiences. Avoid scheduling competing shows in intown neighborhoods this Saturday.

competing_events.name: Grant Park Porchfest
competing_events.pattern: Late March Saturday (3rd annual was 2026-03-21; 2027 is 2027-03-20)
competing_events.notes: Free neighborhood porchfest. Bolo Boys invited verbally by organizer Don for 2027-03-20 (porch/set-time TBD; captured 2026-06-27) — see venue-contacts.json. Avoid competing shows in Grant Park / Beltline this Saturday.

competing_events.name: East Atlanta Strut
competing_events.pattern: Late September Saturday (includes a porchfest component)
competing_events.notes: EAV neighborhood festival with parade, market, and porchfest performances. Strong intown draw. Avoid competing shows in EAV / Grant Park axis this Saturday.

competing_events.name: Inman Park Festival & Tour of Homes
competing_events.notes: Long-running neighborhood festival with parade and tour of homes. Heavy intown turnout. Avoid competing shows in intown neighborhoods this weekend.

competing_events.name: DragonCon
competing_events.notes: Pop-culture convention. Brings ~80k+ attendees to downtown ATL. Audience is convention-focused but spills out to intown bars/restaurants in the evenings. Already referenced in Side Saddle 9/12 invitation copy.

competing_events.name: Shaky Knees Music Festival
competing_events.pattern: Historically May; moved to September starting 2026 (Sep 18–20)
competing_events.notes: Major rock/indie festival. Direct overlap with Bolo Boys' core live-music audience. Already referenced in Side Saddle 9/12 invitation copy as 'shoulder weekend.'

competing_events.name: SweetWater 420 Fest
competing_events.neighborhood: Shirley Clarke Franklin Park (relocated from Centennial Olympic Park)
competing_events.notes: Multi-day music + culture festival from SweetWater Brewing. Mid-April timing.

competing_events.name: Atlanta Pride Festival & Parade
competing_events.pattern: Columbus Day / Indigenous Peoples' Day weekend
competing_events.notes: Major civic event. Heavy intown weekend draw. Parade is Sunday Oct 11. Venues in/near Midtown may benefit from spillover; venues far from Midtown compete for the audience.

competing_events.name: Decatur Book Festival
competing_events.notes: Large independent book festival. Pulls intown east audiences to Decatur. Worth flagging for Wild Heaven Avondale Saturday shows.

competing_events.name: Decatur Craft Beer Festival
competing_events.notes: Beer-festival audience overlaps with brewery-show audiences. Worth flagging for Wild Heaven Avondale Saturday shows.

competing_events.name: Music Midtown
competing_events.pattern: Historically September; on hiatus since 2024 due to Georgia gun-law dispute
competing_events.notes: No 2026 event confirmed. Watch for return announcements — if revived, would be a high-overlap September weekend.

boost_windows.name: Memorial Day weekend
boost_windows.pattern: Last Monday of May (3-day weekend Fri–Mon)
boost_windows.notes: Three-day weekend; people are out and entertainment-seeking. Monday evening shows work especially well because many have the day off — Bolo Boys' Grant Central Pizza 5/25 Memorial Day show is a planned example.

boost_windows.name: Independence Day weekend
boost_windows.pattern: July 4th (Saturday in 2026, so natural 3-day weekend Fri–Sun)
boost_windows.notes: July 4th lands on Saturday in 2026 — full weekend draw. Patio venues especially benefit. Many people travel, so urban-staying-home crowd may be smaller but more concentrated.

boost_windows.name: Labor Day weekend
boost_windows.pattern: First Monday of September (3-day weekend Sat–Mon)
boost_windows.notes: Three-day weekend boost — but overlaps with DragonCon downtown. Net effect for intown patio venues is mixed: locals are out, but the DragonCon-tourist surge skews to downtown/Midtown bars.

boost_windows.name: Halloween weekend
boost_windows.notes: Halloween on a Saturday is the strongest possible alignment. Costume-show opportunity. Bolo Boys' Side Saddle 10/31 Halloween Bash is on the calendar.

boost_windows.name: Thanksgiving week / weekend
boost_windows.pattern: Thanksgiving Thu (Nov 26 in 2026); long weekend Wed–Sun
boost_windows.notes: Wed before Thanksgiving (Nov 25) is historically one of the biggest bar nights of the year. Weekend after is family-heavy and quieter. Pre-Turkey shows the weekend before (mid-Nov Sat) also draw well — Bolo Boys' Side Saddle 11/21 is a planned example.

boost_windows.name: Holiday season
boost_windows.notes: Holiday parties, end-of-year energy, and people in town for family. Side Saddle 12/19 Christmas Party is the planned example.

boost_windows.name: St. Patrick's Day
boost_windows.pattern: March 17 (Tuesday in 2026 — bar boost on the day; the preceding weekend can also lift)
boost_windows.notes: Beer-heavy holiday — strong boost for breweries (Wild Heaven, Cultivation). Weekend before may also lift if bars run themed events.

boost_windows.name: Cinco de Mayo
boost_windows.notes: Modest weeknight boost — venues with Mexican-themed programming see the lift. Not a primary booking driver for Bolo Boys.

boost_windows.name: Spring patio sweet spot
boost_windows.notes: Best Atlanta patio weather of the year. Outdoor venues (Side Saddle, Wild Heaven patio) hit peak attractiveness. Schedule weather-dependent shows here when possible.

boost_windows.name: Fall patio sweet spot
boost_windows.notes: Second-best patio window. Outdoor venues benefit. Side Saddle's October Halloween show + November pre-Turkey show both ride this window.

best_day_patterns: Saturday afternoon (1y anniversary, World Cup Saturday, Halloween)
venue_specific_notes.weather_caveat: Outdoor patio — weather-dependent. Lean spring + fall windows for high-confidence dates.
Indoor + outdoor seating; less weather-dependent than full-patio venues.

best_day_patterns: Monday evening (Grant Ole Opry series — Memorial Day Monday is a planned example)
